questions.text: If two triangles are congruent, then their corresponding parts are:
questions.explanation: This is known as CPCT (Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a criterion for congruence of triangles?
questions.explanation: SSA (Side-Side-Angle) is not a valid congruence rule as it doesn't guarantee uniqueness.

questions.text: In an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite to equal sides are:
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental property of isosceles triangles.

questions.text: Each angle of an equilateral triangle is:
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all three angles are equal: 180/3 = 60°.

questions.text: The sum of any two sides of a triangle is ______ the third side.
questions.explanation: This is known as the Triangle Inequality Theorem.

questions.text: In a right angled triangle, the longest side is:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is opposite the largest angle (90°) and is the longest side.

questions.text: If ∆ABC ≅ ∆PQR, then side AB is equal to:
questions.explanation: Corresponding sides match based on the order of vertices in the congruence statement.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if AB = AC, then angle B is equal to:
questions.explanation: Angles opposite to equal sides are equal.

questions.text: The symbol used for congruence is:
questions.explanation: The symbol ≅ represents 'is congruent to'.

questions.text: If two sides and the included angle of one triangle are equal to two sides and the included angle of another, they are congruent by:
questions.explanation: SAS stands for Side-Angle-Side.

questions.text: In a triangle, the side opposite to the largest angle is the:
questions.explanation: In any triangle, the order of side lengths follows the order of angle measures.

questions.text: ASA congruence rule stands for:
questions.explanation: It requires two angles and the included side.

questions.text: In two right triangles, if the hypotenuse and one side are equal, they are congruent by:
questions.explanation: RHS stands for Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side.

questions.text: If three sides of one triangle are equal to three sides of another triangle, they are congruent by:
questions.explanation: SSS stands for Side-Side-Side.

questions.text: In ∆ABC, if AB = AC and ∠A = 100°, then ∠B is:
A triangle in which all sides are unequal is called:
questions.explanation: Scalene triangles have no equal sides.

questions.text: Can a triangle have sides 2 cm, 3 cm, and 5 cm?
questions.explanation: Sum of two sides (2+3=5) must be strictly greater than the third (5). Here it is equal.

questions.text: In ∆ABC and ∆PQR, AB=PQ, BC=QR and ∠B=∠Q. The triangles are congruent by:
questions.explanation: The equal angle is included between the two equal sides.

questions.text: In a right triangle, if the other two angles are equal, the triangle is:
questions.explanation: The angles would be 90, 45, 45. Two equal angles mean two equal sides.

questions.text: The bisector of the vertical angle of an isosceles triangle ______ the base.
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the angle bisector of the vertex angle also bisects the base perpendicularly.

questions.text: The difference between any two sides of a triangle is ______ the third side.
questions.explanation: Property of triangle inequality.

questions.text: In ∆PQR, if ∠R > ∠Q, then:
questions.explanation: The side opposite the larger angle (PQ) is longer than the side opposite the smaller angle (PR).

questions.text: Two figures are congruent if they have the same:
questions.explanation: Congruence implies identical geometry in every aspect.

questions.text: If two angles and any side of one triangle are equal to two angles and the corresponding side of another triangle, they are congruent by:
questions.explanation: AAS (Angle-Angle-Side) is a variation of ASA.

questions.text: Which side of a right triangle is the longest?
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse, opposite the right angle.

questions.text: A point equidistant from two given points lies on the:
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector of a segment is the locus of all points equidistant from the endpoints.

questions.text: The medians of an equilateral triangle are:
questions.explanation: By symmetry, all three medians are identical in length in an equilateral triangle.

questions.text: In ∆ABC, if ∠A=40° and ∠B=60°, which is the longest side?
questions.explanation: ∠C = 180 - (40+60) = 80°. Side opposite ∠C (which is AB) is the longest.

questions.text: Congruent triangles have ______ areas.
questions.explanation: Identity implies equal area.

questions.text: Can a triangle have two right angles?
options: Only if it is very large
Only if it is very small
questions.explanation: Two right angles would sum to 180°, leaving 0° for the third angle, which is impossible.

questions.text: The perimeter of a triangle is the ______ of its sides.
questions.explanation: Definition of perimeter.

questions.text: If ABC is an isosceles triangle with AB=AC, and AD is a median, then AD is:
questions.explanation: Median to the base of an isosceles triangle is also an altitude.

questions.text: Internal bisectors of angles of a triangle meet at:
questions.explanation: Standard definition in geometry.

questions.text: Altitudes of a triangle meet at:
questions.explanation: Standard definition in geometry.

questions.text: Medians of a triangle meet at:
questions.explanation: Standard definition in geometry.

questions.text: The centroid divides each median in the ratio:
questions.explanation: The centroid is 2/3 of the way from the vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.text: If ∆ABC ≅ ∆FED under the correspondence ABC ↔ FED, write the parts of ∆ABC that correspond to DE:
questions.explanation: BC corresponds to ED (or DE) based on vertex positions.

questions.text: Two circles are congruent if they have the same:
questions.explanation: Radii determine the size of a circle.

questions.text: In ∆ABC, if AB=AC and ∠B=50°, then ∠A is:
Angles of an equilateral triangle are ______ to each other.
questions.explanation: All are 60 degrees.
